originalTitle: Middle East crisis live: US launches new wave of strikes; UAE says two tankers in strait of Hormuz hit by Iranian missiles
summary: US military says latest strikes will ‘continue imposing a heavy cost on Iranian forces and degrade their ability to attack innocent civilians and commercial shipping’ in strait of Hormuz
content: Alarm sirens have sounded in Bahrain and people should head to the nearest safe location, the interior ministry is saying on social media. The country earlier accused Iran of targeting civilians with its latest attacks on Bahrain, after Tehran said it had struck US military facilities and infrastructure there. Donald Trump’s announcement that the US will start charging ships for safe passage through the strait of Hormuz comes after his administration has until now insisted the waterway should remain open to all without tolls – as it was before the US and Israel attacked Iran in late February. Any attempt by Washington or Tehran to charge fees would violate global norms on freedom of navigation and raise tensions, likely causing further economic disruption far beyond the region The price of Brent crude oil, the international standard, rose 7.8% to $81.92 a barrel on Monday, although it is still well below the nearly $120 reached at the height of the war. The US launched its third consecutive night of strikes on Iran hours after Trump announced the tolling plan and said Washington would also reinstate its maritime blockade on Iran. He said: We’re going to hit them very hard tonight and we’re going to hit them hard tomorrow – and there’s not a damn thing they can do about it. There’s more in our latest full report here: More now on Donald Trump saying a deal with Iran was still possible despite the latest outbreak of hostilities. “Yeah, I think a deal is possible. Sure I do,” the president is quoted as telling reporters in the Oval Office. We had a deal with them two days ago and then they said ‘Oh we can’t make that deal. We have to negotiate it further.’ Trump also said there might be Iranian drones in Cuba. “If they do have that, and they might very well have that, we’ll take care of it,” ⁠the president reportedly said, adding: We’re not going to have a problem. We’re not going to allow that to happen so ‌it could be that they are storing some. We’re looking into it now. The United Arab Emirates is saying Iranian cruise missiles have targeted two UAE tankers in the strait of Hormuz and that one crew member has been killed and eight injured. The defence ministry said the attack in Omani territorial waters caused damage and fires onboard both ships but they had been brought under control. It said in a statement posted on X: The Ministry of Defense condemns this brazen attack, which constitutes a serious violation and a clear breach of international law, threatening the security and stability of the region. The ministry named the ships as the Mombasa and the Bahia and said the attack occurred in the southern passage of the strait. The crew member killed on the Mombasa was Indian, while of the eight injured six were Indian and two Ukrainian, it said. Four of those were seriously injured. Donald Trump is reportedly saying he thinks a deal with Iran is still possible. He also says the US is knocking out Iran’s offensive capability and is carrying out another major attack on Monday evening (it’s now 6.10pm Eastern Time). The president is also quoted as saying the US wants to be reimbursed for protecting countries it is helping in the strait of Hormuz. We’ll bring you more on his comments shortly. As mentioned earlier, Trump today announced the US will start charging fees on ships transiting through the vital energy route, saying there will be 20% fee to cover the costs of providing security for vessels. US Central Command said that at 4.45pm ET it had begun a third consecutive night of strikes against Iran at Donald Trump’s direction. “These strikes will continue imposing a heavy cost on Iranian forces and degrade their ability to attack innocent civilians and commercial shipping in the Strait of Hormuz,” Centcom said in a post on X. Donald Trump has said the US plans to carry out further strikes against Iran, with the memorandum of understanding in a shambles after fighting reignited between the two sides last week. “We’re going to hit them very hard tonight, and we’re going to hit them hard tomorrow,” he said in a radio interview. Trump added that the MoU “didn’t mean much” because it had been a test, which Iran had not honoured. It came hours after Trump claimed that the strait of Hormuz is open and will “remain” open “with or without Iran”, and said the US will reinstate its blockade of Iranian ports. The US president also announced that the US would start charging fees on ships transiting through the narrow waterway, claiming the US would levy a 20% fee “for any and all costs necessary” to provide security and safety for vessels. The US military started preparing to resume blockading ships heading to and exiting Iranian ports from 4pm ET on Tuesday, US Central Command said, after Trump’s announcement. More on that here. Iran’s foreign minister Abbas Araghchi mocked Trump over his vow to impose hefty charges on all cargo shipped through the strait of Hormuz, saying Iran would charge a lower rate. “POTUS is absolutely right. Whoever provides secure and safe passage of commercial vessels through the strait of Hormuz should be compensated for this service. Iran has always been the GUARDIAN of the Strait and will remain so FOREVER. 20% is of course too much. We will be fair,” Tehran’s top diplomat wrote, in a social media post. The International Maritime Organization (IMO) said that passage through the strait of Hormuz “should remain free of any tolls and charges, in accordance with international law”. In a statement, the IMO Council reaffirmed its commitment to protecting vital shipping lanes, defending freedom of navigation, upholding international law and ensuring the safety of seafarers. It also condemned attacks on civilian commercial ships in and around the strait and called for de-escalation of tensions in the region. “The right of transit passage through straits used for international navigation should not be threatened, impeded, denied, hampered, impaired or suspended,” it said. chineseTitle: 中东危机直播：美国发动新一波打击；阿联酋称霍尔木兹海峡两艘油轮被伊朗导弹击中
chineseSummary: 美国军方表示，最新的袭击将“继续给伊朗军队造成沉重代价，并削弱他们在霍尔木兹海峡攻击无辜平民和商业航运的能力”
chineseContent: 巴林内政部在社交媒体上表示，巴林的警报已经拉响，人们应该前往最近的安全地点。在德黑兰表示袭击了巴林的美国军事设施和基础设施后，该国早些时候指责伊朗最近对巴林的袭击针对平民。唐纳德·特朗普宣布美国将开始对安全通过霍尔木兹海峡的船只收费，此前他的政府一直坚持该水道应继续向所有人开放且不收取通行费 — — 就像美国和以色列在二月底袭击伊朗之前那样。华盛顿或德黑兰收取费用的任何尝试都将违反全球航行自由规范并加剧紧张局势，可能会导致远远超出该地区的进一步经济破坏。 国际标准布伦特原油价格周一上涨 7.8％，至每桶 81.92 美元，尽管仍远低于战争最激烈时达到的近 120 美元。在特朗普宣布通行费计划数小时后，美国连续第三晚对伊朗发动袭击，并表示华盛顿还将恢复对伊朗的海上封锁。他说：今晚我们将严厉打击他们，明天我们也会严厉打击他们——而他们对此却无能为力。我们最新的完整报告中有更多内容：唐纳德·特朗普表示，尽管最近爆发了敌对行动，但仍有可能与伊朗达成协议。 “是的，我认为达成协议是可能的。当然我愿意，”总统在椭圆形办公室对记者说。两天前我们与他们达成协议，然后他们说‘哦，我们无法达成这笔交易。我们必须进一步谈判。”特朗普还表示，古巴可能有伊朗无人机。 “如果他们确实拥有这个，而且他们很可能拥有那个，我们就会
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
